reason: path.resolve()+startsWith(uploadsDir) blocks all traversal — safe route correctly rejects ../

note: Modern Node.js (v14+) strips the null byte before path resolution — path.join treats the payload as a literal filename containing %00, which does not escape the traversal; the vulnerable join+readFile still fires. Old Node behavior (pre-v0.12) would truncate at the null byte. Outcome: fires on all supported Node versions because join does not decode %00, so the traversal sequences (../../etc/passwd) are still present in the resolved path.
